subject: Re: Relion CTF
body:
Hi Dimitry,

Thank you very much for the clarification! 

Best,

Jonathan

El jueves, 22 de julio de 2021 a las 16:24:26 UTC+2, teg...@gmail.com 
escribió:

> Hi Jonathan,
>
> This is the expected output format, Relion shouldn't have any problems 
> reading it. The CTF is stored in an FFTW-like format where only one half 
> (width / 2 + 1) is kept due to Hermitian symmetry and the center is shifted 
> to the first element.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Tuesday, July 20, 2021 at 6:21:18 PM UTC+2 jonathang...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi all,
>>
>> I am recently working with warp. I am pretty new to it.
>>
>> I was wondering something about the CTF obtained from the "reconstruct 
>> sub-particles."
>>
>> The CTF volume obtained has a dimension of:
>>
>> Number of columns, rows, sections 41      80      80
>>
>> Instead, the images have these dimensions:
>>
>> Number of columns, rows, sections 80      80      80
>>
>>
>> I am attaching a screenshot of the volume opened with chimera. 
>>
>> Is this data the usual one? If not, what could be the problem.
>>
>> Thanks all,
>>
>> Jonathan
>>
>>
